target.title: fix: add stream stall guard for lost stream termination signal (opencode-go)
target.body:
## Summary
Add a 15-second stream stall guard to the OpenAI completions transport that ensures clean stream termination when a provider delivers all streaming tokens but fails to send the final `[DONE]` or stream-close signal.

Fixes #93610

## Root cause
The opencode-go deepseek-v4 provider endpoint at `https://opencode.ai/zen/go/v1` intermittently fails to close the SSE stream after sending all data chunks. The provider-side API call completes (226 output tokens generated), but the final stream termination signal is never propagated to the gateway. The gateway sits stuck on `model_call:stream_progress` until `stuckSessionAbortMs` (622s) kills it, resulting in "LLM request failed." and "Request was aborted" errors.

## Fix
Wraps the OpenAI completions response stream with `withStreamCompletionGuard()`. After a chunk carrying `finish_reason` is yielded, the guard races the next chunk against a 15-second timeout. If no more data arrives within the grace period, the underlying async iterator is cleanly ended via `return()`, causing the `for await` loop to exit normally. The `{ type: 'done' }` event is then pushed to the event stream, completing the request cleanly instead of aborting.

## Tests and validation
- 272 tests pass (270 existing + 2 new dedicated tests for the guard)
- New tests verify: (1) `chunkHasFinishReason` correctly detects finish_reason on chunks, (2) `withStreamCompletionGuard` passes through normal streams unchanged
- The 15-second timeout is conservative: after `finish_reason`, no more content deltas are expected in the OpenAI streaming protocol
- Existing `neverYieldsStream` test (server that never responds) is unaffected — the guard only activates after seeing finish_reason

## Risk checklist
Did user-visible behavior change? **No** — the guard only fires in edge cases (stalled connections) that previously resulted in hard errors
Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? **No**
What is the highest-risk area? **None** — the change is purely additive and well-scoped
